rationale: The previous attempt's bare `assert(post1_self_.wf())` failed because the ensures has the shape `(antecedent) ==> det_equal(...)` — at the top of the body, the antecedent is the implication's hypothesis, not a fact in scope. Wrapping every cross-run derivation inside `if (full_antecedent) { ... }` makes each antecedent conjunct a hypothesis inside the branch, so the post1/post2 forall-quantifiers can be co-triggered and the cross-side pointwise equalities (procs, containers off `owning`, endpoints, threads off r1/r2) plus the domain / page_closure / owning-scheduler equalities go through; by the WP rule for `if`, those facts are propagated as `antecedent ==> ...` lemmas to the ensures-implication check.
